bead.title: Audit deleted HELIX artifact types for restoration or retirement
bead.description:
<context-digest>
<principles>Design for Change · Design for Simplicity · Validate Your Work · Make Intent Explicit · Prefer Reversible Decisions</principles>
<concerns>hugo-hextra | demo-asciinema</concerns>
<practices>Theme version: Hextra v0.12.1 — pinned in website/go.mod · Hugo version: 0.159.2 extended — pinned in .github/workflows/pages.yml · Current demos: helix-quickstart (full lifecycle), helix-concerns (drift detection), helix-evolve (requirement threading) · Agent harness: ddx agent run --harness claude — not claude -p</practices>
<governing>workflows/README.md — A child review bead exists for each deleted referenced artifact type</governing>
</context-digest>

Review the deleted HELIX artifact types that are still referenced directly or indirectly in current workflow docs. For each artifact type, evaluate its original intent against current HELIX state, decide whether it should be restored, superseded, or retired, and define the minimum acceptable prompt/template bar if it remains part of the workflow surface.
bead.acceptance: A child review bead exists for each deleted referenced artifact type; each child is scoped to determine restore vs supersede vs retire and the minimum prompt/template quality bar for that artifact intent
